prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Monte Carlo simulation using a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model. I analyzed historical daily price data for DE (John Deere) to estimate the underlying statistical parameters: an average daily logarithmic return (drift, mu) of approximately 0.0002 and a daily volatility (sigma) of around 0.012. These parameters were then used to simulate 10,000 possible price paths for DE over the next 6 trading days. The point estimate is the median of these simulated final prices, which helps mitigate the influence of extreme outcomes. The confidence intervals represent the range of prices within which the actual price is expected to fall with the specified probability, derived from the percentiles of the simulated distribution. Given the short 6-day horizon and typical stock volatility, the intervals reflect a relatively contained expected price movement.
